batches.heading: Social relation, standing, and personal boundaries
System fit, equivalence, and dependable performance
Work timing, status, capability, and answerability
Medical course, transmission, and treatment response
Participation, cooperation, contribution, and mutual aid
Care, affection, regard, and relational repair
Learning, practice, guidance, and sustained effort
Relation, boundaries, and remaining practical acts
Personhood, generations, kinship, and social belonging
Time, sequence, natural stations, and recurrence
Plants, animals, and the living world
Dwelling, vessels, food, clothing, and light
Space, orientation, boundaries, and physical form

candidates.concept: hollow interior structure
candidates.summary: An empty interior says what is hollow without treating every empty vessel or room as a material structure.

candidates.concept: absorption of liquid or other material
candidates.summary: The verb puts the receiving body in subject position and distinguishes material taken within from liquid merely present on a surface or moving through.

candidates.concept: porous interior structure
candidates.summary: Many small empty interiors can describe the visible arrangement, while a dedicated quality may become useful in soil, textile, building, or filter work.
candidates.revisit_when: connected material practice repeatedly needs porosity itself rather than permeability to a named substance

candidates.concept: dense packing or scientific density
candidates.placement: base, Systems, Ecological, or exact source material
candidates.summary: Everyday uses divide among thick body, high concentration, and many things in little space; scientific density adds a defined magnitude and method.
candidates.revisit_when: connected material or scientific writing repeatedly needs one stable relation that existing thickness, concentration, packing, and source measurements cannot express cleanly

candidates.concept: permeability to a named substance
candidates.placement: base clause, with exact technical criteria in source material
candidates.summary: Permeability depends on what passes, under which conditions, so the ordinary clause names the substance, material, and possible passage.

candidates.concept: waterproofness
candidates.placement: base clause, with exact ratings in source material
candidates.summary: A clause saying that water cannot enter or pass through leaves the claimed substance and practical condition visible.
expressions: phialu muo muralo po ma koema

candidates.concept: temperature, humidity, and illumination as measured parameters
candidates.placement: Systems or Ecological, with exact values in source material
candidates.summary: Everyday quality words already cover hot, wet, and bright; technical work may still need neutral parameter nouns.
candidates.revisit_when: a connected technical scenario repeatedly names the measured parameter apart from its value and instrument

candidates.concept: area as a neutral magnitude
candidates.summary: The measured kind can name the relevant surface or place before `ponalu` supplies its size, while exact values stay in source material.

candidates.concept: volume as occupied spatial magnitude
candidates.summary: Overall size already handles ordinary occupied extent, and the size of a space can be named directly without confusing it with a container's technical capacity.

candidates.concept: weight as a neutral physical magnitude
candidates.summary: The noun lets speakers compare or measure physical weight before judging an object heavy or light and without borrowing burden or technical load.

candidates.concept: mass as a scientific magnitude distinct from weight
candidates.summary: Ordinary handling now has neutral weight, while scientific mass requires a stable technical distinction, method, and source value.
candidates.revisit_when: connected scientific or engineering writing repeatedly needs to discuss mass apart from weight and exact source notation

candidates.concept: scientific basic-taste categories
candidates.placement: culinary or Medical use, with exact taxonomies source-bound
candidates.summary: Ordinary flavours can use source nouns and response clauses without pretending that one scientific inventory is universal.
candidates.revisit_when: connected culinary or medical writing repeatedly needs a stable taste distinction beyond source description

candidates.concept: itch, numbness, tingling, and dizziness
candidates.summary: A person can name four familiar sensations before anyone adds a broader bodily condition or clinical conclusion.

candidates.concept: decorate or add applied ornament
candidates.summary: The verb names the addition of visible ornament and keeps design as a separate act.

candidates.concept: symmetry and asymmetry
candidates.placement: base, Work, or exact technical description
candidates.summary: Ordinary clauses can compare corresponding parts, but design or technical work may need symmetry as a recurring property.
candidates.revisit_when: a connected design, craft, or technical scenario repeats the relation often enough that comparison clauses become cumbersome

candidates.concept: style and characteristic aesthetic form
candidates.summary: The noun names a recognisable manner shared across works while keeping pattern, design, shape, and aesthetic judgement distinct.

candidates.concept: straight as an ordinary shape quality
candidates.summary: The adjective separates an uncurved course or extended form from a direct route or steady movement.

candidates.concept: round as an ordinary shape quality
candidates.summary: The existing circle noun already describes a circular form through the ordinary noun-describes rule, and active text has used the expression naturally.

candidates.concept: cruel conduct and cruelty
candidates.summary: The adjective judges knowingly or callously caused avoidable suffering without declaring a permanent kind of person.

candidates.concept: generic conflict and direct roots for violence, fighting, attack, defence, hunting, and killing
candidates.summary: Phi refuses to make violent acts and roles ordinary vocabulary while retaining direct ways to name danger, harm, injury, coercion, protection, testimony, responsibility, redress, and repair.
candidates.decision: Do not coin these roots in base vocabulary or a module. Describe mechanisms and effects with established Phi words, and preserve exact violent terminology beside Phi when faithful source wording is required.

candidates.concept: sustained interest or engagement
candidates.summary: The adjective keeps interest apart from curiosity and directed attention.

candidates.concept: guilt, anxiety, disgust, excitement, relief, frustration, and boredom
candidates.summary: Seven direct forms expand ordinary self-report beyond the core lesson. Each entry keeps the feeling apart from the judgement, event, or bodily condition that may accompany it.

candidates.concept: dirty, contaminated, spoiled, and rotten
candidates.summary: Base `hureki` names physical dirt. Existing clean, contaminant, spoil, and decompose entries keep practical cleanliness, unexpected presence, lost fitness, and material breakdown separate; active or completed decomposition supplies ordinary rotting without a moralised rotten root.

candidates.concept: rice and tea
candidates.summary: The two nouns cover ordinary rice and tea, while exact cultivars, blends, and trade names remain with their sources.

candidates.concept: nut
candidates.summary: The registered compound names a fruit by its hard stone-like shell and is already used in an active text.

candidates.concept: legal or certified accessibility
candidates.summary: Phi can describe the practical access relation but cannot silently inherit a jurisdiction's legal test.
candidates.source_rule: Keep the standard, measurement, decision, and legal category in their exact source form; use Phi for the access conditions and effects around them.

candidates.concept: standards conformance, protocol support, and certified performance
candidates.summary: A practical Phi quality cannot replace the version, test method, evidence, or issuing authority.
candidates.source_rule: Preserve the exact standard, protocol, version, result, or certificate and describe its practical system relation in Phi.

candidates.concept: occupation titles, credentials, licences, and legal liability
candidates.placement: Work or Commons plus exact source material
candidates.summary: These categories draw their force from a named institution or legal order.
candidates.source_rule: Keep the exact title, credential, issuer, scope, validity, or legal rule beside the Phi account of work, qualification, authority, or answerability.

candidates.concept: indicated care and exact contraindication classes
candidates.summary: A general inverse of contraindicated would hide the source, strength, and grounds of a clinical recommendation.
candidates.source_rule: Keep the exact recommendation or classification with its source and criteria; use Phi to state expected benefit, alternatives, reasons, and the person's decision.

candidates.concept: amnesia, dementia, impairment, and exact cognitive assessment
candidates.summary: Ordinary memory and understanding words cannot carry a diagnosis or test result by themselves.
candidates.source_rule: Preserve the diagnosis, test, classification, and source; use Phi to describe the person's reported experience and observed abilities without replacing those records.

candidates.concept: lose and misplace a thing
candidates.summary: The verb reports an unintentionally missing possession or location. Deliberate release, abandonment, contest results, and wider bereavement keep their own accounts.

candidates.concept: named meditation traditions, methods, and lineages
candidates.placement: base practice vocabulary plus exact source names
candidates.summary: One broad Phi meditation verb does not erase the histories and methods of named traditions.
candidates.source_rule: Retain the tradition, method, teacher, or lineage name in its source form and use Phi to describe the practice being undertaken.

candidates.concept: meet as a verb
candidates.summary: Phi does not turn a thing noun into its deed. The new verb names an encounter by plan or chance, while `lona` remains the gathering or meeting event.

candidates.concept: emergence, correlation, inference strength, and further responsibility distinctions
candidates.placement: Philosophical Reasoning, Systems, or shared base
candidates.summary: The first philosophical pass named these as later questions rather than completed omissions.
candidates.revisit_when: new philosophical arguments or systems explanations need one of the distinctions repeatedly and the existing relations become clumsy or ambiguous

candidates.concept: feedforward and later systems relations
candidates.summary: The first Systems pass retained feedback and control but left feedforward and other less common relations for connected technical use.
candidates.revisit_when: an original system explanation needs anticipatory control often enough that ordinary input, model, and control clauses obscure the relation

candidates.concept: money, prices, wages, debt, profit, taxation, budgets, and monetary accounting
candidates.placement: no Phi lexical roots; exact source records remain available beside a Phi account
candidates.summary: The later canon decision places the clock, ruler, and price tag under the same refusal. Exchange, giving, receiving, sharing, obligation, extraction, and livelihood keep their own non-monetary relations.
candidates.decision: Phi has no monetary roots or internal monetary accounting. Exact economic records remain in their source form when people need to discuss them.

candidates.concept: motivation, explanatory cause, justification, and purpose
candidates.placement: base clauses and existing reasoning vocabulary
candidates.summary: English why can ask what moved a person, what caused an event, what an action was for, or what justifies a claim. Phi keeps those relations separate so that one answer cannot quietly stand in for another.

candidates.concept: proof and certainty in ordinary claims
candidates.placement: base reasoning words with Philosophical Reasoning where needed
candidates.summary: Ordinary proof is better stated through the evidence, reasons, inference, or refutation that settles a particular point. Knowledge and confidence then report different stances towards the result rather than becoming a portable certificate of certainty.

candidates.concept: formal proofs, exact probability values, odds, and named logical or epistemic standards
candidates.placement: exact source material beside a Phi account
candidates.summary: A formal result depends on its notation, axioms, method, reference class, and stated standard. Phi can discuss the argument and evidence without recoding the identity that makes the result reproducible.
candidates.source_rule: Formal proofs keep their notation, axioms, and named logic in source form. Probability statements keep their value, odds, reference class, and method identifier. Phi states what is claimed, how the result was obtained, and how it enters the present decision.

candidates.concept: favourable and harmful luck, chance contribution, and coincidence
candidates.placement: base nouns, modifiers, and temporal clauses
candidates.summary: Base `soleha` identifies the unplanned part of an outcome, and a modifier states how it mattered. A coincidence can place events in the same moment or relation without adding a causal claim.

candidates.concept: separate Phi roots for funeral, wedding rite, baptism, initiation, and other prescribed rites
candidates.placement: described ceremonies under the single base noun `thorea`
candidates.summary: A dedicated root would make one inherited division of ceremonial life look universal. Phi instead says what the ceremony marks and leaves its form with the participants and tradition.
candidates.decision: `thorea` remains Phi's only ceremony word. The occasion or relation can be described, as in `lumeo thorea`; prescribed rites receive no separate roots.

candidates.concept: exact ceremony and ritual names, inherited formulas, calendars, offices, and lineage identities
candidates.placement: source identity beside a Phi description
candidates.summary: The general words for ceremony, ritual, custom, tradition, and festival allow discussion without replacing the identities that particular communities maintain.
candidates.source_rule: A rite or festival keeps its exact name and inherited formula in source form. Calendars, ritual offices, lineage names, and tradition-specific classifications do the same. Phi may describe the actions, participants, timing, meaning, and consent around them.

candidates.concept: named souls, spirits, magical beings, divine agents, doctrines, and metaphysical classifications
candidates.placement: base metaphysical vocabulary with exact source identities
candidates.summary: Phi can affirm, question, deny, narrate, or compare a metaphysical account. Exact names and doctrinal distinctions remain with the people and sources that establish what those entities or categories are.
candidates.source_rule: Named spirits, souls, deities, and magical beings remain in their source form. Doctrines, cosmologies, afterlife states, and tradition-specific metaphysical categories do the same. Phi states the speaker's stance, evidence, relation, and interpretation.

candidates.concept: fate, destiny, and providence as general claims
candidates.placement: base future, purpose, modality, luck, and claim vocabulary
candidates.summary: A speaker can claim that a future is necessary, that a life or event has a purpose, or that chance shaped an outcome. Keeping those claims separate avoids turning fate into an unexplained mixture of necessity, purpose, and luck.

candidates.concept: revelation, prophecy, omen, and other claimed signs of what is hidden or future
candidates.placement: base vision, claim, sign, future, and evidential vocabulary
candidates.summary: A vision can be reported as a vision, a prophecy as a future claim, and an omen as a sign under a stated interpretation. The evidential keeps the claimed route visible, while exact inherited categories remain source material.

candidates.concept: exact laws, legal rights, offences, remedies, judgments, contracts, and institutional justice procedures
candidates.placement: Commons vocabulary beside exact legal source material
candidates.summary: Phi can discuss fairness, authority, accountability, redress, review, jurisdiction, and normative rights. The legal wording and status that decide what a document or institution actually recognises must remain exact.
candidates.source_rule: Statutes, legal rights and offences, remedies, judgments, and contracts remain in their source form. Jurisdiction, filing status, deadline, and procedure name do the same. Phi discusses the source and interpretation, then the effect and consequences for the people involved.

candidates.concept: importance, priority, merit, deservingness, dignity, and worth
candidates.placement: base value and worth vocabulary with Work priority where needed
candidates.summary: Valuing is an act, worth is a claimed property, and priority orders attention under a purpose. Merit and deservingness can state the relevant conduct, contribution, need, criterion, or responsibility instead of ranking a whole person.

candidates.concept: mental health, reproductive health, sexuality, gender, abuse, and coercive care
candidates.placement: base, Medical, or another sensitive domain profile
candidates.summary: The first Medical pass reserved these areas because they need language for self-description, consent, harm, care, and source-defined classifications without treating any one experience as the norm.
candidates.revisit_when: separate scenarios are ready for each area, with autonomy, varied lived experience, refusal, power, and exact source terminology kept visible

candidates.concept: neutral retraction of a claim or proposal
candidates.summary: Retraction changes the speaker's present commitment while leaving the earlier words in the record.

candidates.concept: nausea
candidates.summary: The noun names a felt possibility of vomiting rather than sickness as a whole or the event of vomiting.

candidates.concept: sneezing, hiccups, and belching
candidates.summary: Three ordinary verbs distinguish a sneeze's nasal burst, a hiccup's caught breath, and gas released in a belch.

candidates.concept: lick and suck
candidates.summary: One verb follows the tongue across a surface; the other follows inward pressure made by the mouth.

candidates.concept: brief physical impact
candidates.summary: A general impact root gathers contact, displacement, damage, and bodily injury under an unmarked verb whose ordinary reach includes interpersonal blows.
candidates.decision: Retire patore. Forceful contact uses kema palo, while pesa, pukeri, or kaworu states movement or consequence when the event establishes it.

candidates.concept: peace-linguistic scope and retention of patore
candidates.placement: base expressions and existing result words
candidates.summary: Forceful contact, displacement, material damage, and bodily injury remain separate claims, so ordinary Phi selects the distinction the event establishes.

candidates.concept: physical alignment towards a selected point or object
candidates.summary: `wea` (TOWARD) names the selected reference and `rato` (turn) changes an object's facing. The construction covers physical alignment without importing the combat-shaped extensions of aim or target.

candidates.concept: puncture or make an opening with a point
candidates.summary: The verb separates a point-made hole from edge-driven cutting, digging, and sewing.

candidates.concept: dissolve through a medium
candidates.summary: The verb names dispersion through a medium without treating dissolution as mixing, melting, or disappearance.

candidates.concept: English-shaped material metaphors in active texts
candidates.placement: text review rather than new vocabulary by default
candidates.summary: Breaking into talk, digging into books, heavy trouble, and similar phrases need judgement as Phi images rather than silent definition changes.
candidates.revisit_when: the planned active-text vocabulary review reaches each cited passage

candidates.concept: swell and shrink
candidates.summary: Two deliberately distant forms cover opposite changes in bodily or material extent, with cause and diagnosis left to the surrounding account.

candidates.concept: wither or wilt
candidates.summary: The verb names visible loss of turgor before dryness or death has necessarily occurred.

candidates.concept: biological reproduction and deliberate breeding
candidates.placement: base, Ecological, or a reproductive-health vocabulary set
candidates.summary: Fertility names capacity and birth names an event; reproduction and selective breeding introduce other relations.
candidates.revisit_when: ecological, husbandry, reproductive-health, or kinship scenarios need these relations in connected use

candidates.concept: pollination, pruning, weeding, and fertilising
candidates.summary: Existing verbs can describe what moves, is cut, is removed, or is supplied, while repeated specialist use may favour direct words.
candidates.revisit_when: a garden, farm, orchard, or ecological-care scenario uses the acts often enough to decide their learning value

candidates.concept: try or attempt an action
candidates.summary: The verb reports an action undertaken without claiming completion, persistence, or success.

candidates.concept: courses, curricula, schools, grades, and educational standing
candidates.placement: a possible Learning and Knowledge Practice module plus exact source material
candidates.summary: A lesson composes readily, while institutional categories depend on their programme and issuer.
candidates.revisit_when: the Learning and Knowledge Practice profile receives connected scenarios or the book needs recurring institutional education vocabulary

candidates.concept: search or seek
candidates.summary: The verb names sustained action towards finding something without claiming that the search succeeds.

candidates.concept: page, ink, letter, and message
candidates.summary: Page and message receive direct nouns. Writing dye supplies ink, writing sign supplies a written character, and writing message supplies a letter without reproducing the English homonym.

candidates.concept: applause
candidates.summary: Hands touching each other in rhythm describe the gesture; appreciation, celebration, or encouragement states why the gathering responds.
expressions: miona wi manuwe roe telui wiso palo

candidates.concept: cover and uncover
candidates.summary: The new verb names the physical overlying act. Existing `phae` (open) removes or moves a cover when view or access returns, while closing and hiding keep their own purposes.

candidates.concept: bee, wolf, deer, bear, frog, spider, cow, and goat
candidates.summary: All eight familiar animals receive direct roots for ordinary ecological description, household life, and stories. Exact species, breeds, and technical classifications remain source-specific where needed.

candidates.concept: joint, elbow, wrist, ankle, hip, toe, eyelid, and nostril
candidates.summary: A general body-joint noun supports the smaller landmarks, while hip keeps a direct regional word and the transparent subdivisions remain visible.

candidates.concept: brain
candidates.summary: The organ now has its own noun without turning head, mind, memory, and thought into synonyms.

candidates.concept: throat
candidates.summary: The ordinary internal region is now distinct from the external neck and from exact clinical structures.

candidates.concept: lip, jaw, cheek, and forehead
candidates.summary: Four common landmarks now support speech, eating, expression, touch, pain, and care without fresh descriptive puzzles.

candidates.concept: waist, pelvis, and buttocks
candidates.summary: The lower torso now has ordinary regional nouns for clothing, posture, gait, pain, and non-contact care.

candidates.concept: saliva, urine, and faeces
candidates.summary: Daily care, sanitation, childhood, and illness can now name these substances without euphemism or a medical module.

candidates.concept: mucus, body fat, and flesh
candidates.summary: Mucus and fat keep their precise substance nouns. Flesh gathers the body's soft material into an ordinary whole without becoming one tissue or a food category.

candidates.concept: sexual and reproductive anatomy
candidates.placement: base and Medical, decided by ordinary autonomy and care needs
candidates.summary: This field must name bodies without treating one anatomy as the default or requiring a diagnosis before a body part can be spoken.
candidates.revisit_when: a dedicated scenario set tests self-description, consent, sexual health, reproduction, pain, and care across varied bodies

candidates.concept: named bones, vessels, nerves, glands, and exact anatomical classifications
candidates.placement: Medical plus exact biomedical source material
candidates.summary: Base vocabulary need not reproduce an atlas, but recurring care or study may reveal specialist words worth learning.
candidates.revisit_when: a care, study, or public-health scenario repeatedly needs one structure beyond ordinary body-region language

candidates.concept: today, yesterday, tomorrow, and every day
candidates.summary: The day stays audible while a demonstrative, temporal noun, or quantifier locates it. Four short expressions cover the ordinary daily references without a second set of roots.

candidates.concept: always, never, often, rarely, and sometimes
candidates.summary: Frequency remains a claim over time or moments, so the size of the claim stays in the utterance instead of hiding inside an adverb.

candidates.concept: relative proximity, earliness, lateness, and simultaneity
candidates.placement: base grammar and expressions, with optional module vocabulary for organised work
candidates.summary: Near or far time, before or after a reference, and occurrence within one span state the useful relation. Work that is deliberately spread across different times may use module adjective `thunesi` (asynchronous).

candidates.concept: age, lifetime, birthdays, anniversaries, and larger spans
candidates.summary: A person holds counted years; a life has a period; a birth has a day; and a returning observance can name its event, annual cycle, or ceremony. These relations remain clearer than one broad anniversary root.
expressions: mia wi phoi ta shao torua phelu

candidates.concept: calendar, history, chronology, and timeline
candidates.placement: base expressions plus separately preserved source records
candidates.summary: A time record, a story or record of the past, ordinal position, and before-after relations distinguish the artefact from the account it carries. A named external calendar or chronology keeps its own source form.

candidates.concept: exact clock readings, clock units, time zones, and named calendar identifiers
candidates.placement: separate source material with a Phi account
candidates.summary: Phi's natural stations and calendar spans support ordinary temporal speech, while an exact external value retains the notation and standard that make it usable.
candidates.source_rule: The complete clock reading, time zone, named calendar date, and exact unit notation remain outside the Phi passage. A Phi account may identify the record, its purpose, and its relation to an event without replacing data needed for coordination, care, accessibility, testimony, or safety.

candidates.concept: soil, ground, dry land, coast, and beach
candidates.summary: `muila` already covers earth as soil, ground, and land, while `kerime` covers the water's edge and coastal strip. Dryness and loose beach material remain visible when those details matter.

candidates.concept: desert, cliff, and volcano
candidates.summary: Three common landforms need more than a temporary condition or a picturesque approximation. The new roots keep a desert distinct from drought, a cliff distinct from its edge, and a quiet volcano distinct from a burning mountain.

candidates.concept: tide
candidates.summary: A recurring coastal cycle affects travel and shoreline work. It also determines access, gathering, and safety often enough to deserve a direct noun. Its entry keeps waves and currents separate.

candidates.concept: current, glacier, waterfall, groundwater, and wetland
candidates.placement: base expressions with an ecological-module term where needed
candidates.summary: Movement through water takes more than one form. Existing expressions distinguish current and waterfall, while frozen flow, subsurface water, and wet habitat keep their defining relation audible. A technical classification can remain in its source record beside the Phi account.

candidates.concept: planet
candidates.summary: Sun, star, moon, and Earth left the ordinary celestial inventory without a word for the class Earth belongs to. `nowari` supplies that class while named planets keep their source identities.

candidates.concept: horizon, atmosphere, and humidity
candidates.summary: The visible sky can have an apparent boundary, ordinary atmosphere is air around a place or world, and humidity is moisture in that air. Exact scientific layers and measurements remain separately identified when needed.

candidates.concept: hail, drizzle, rainstorm, snowstorm, and thunderstorm
candidates.summary: Familiar weather subtypes remain transparent combinations of material, intensity, and event. Their exact thresholds and named warning categories come from the weather source in use.

candidates.concept: narrow geological and coastal landform classes
candidates.placement: base, ecological module, or exact source term according to use
candidates.summary: Bay, gulf, strait, canyon, gorge, delta, dune, and similar classes differ in which shape, process, or local name matters. Existing land and water relations can carry ordinary description until one distinction repeatedly needs a root.
candidates.revisit_when: an active navigation, geological, ecological, or literary scenario repeatedly loses a landform distinction that existing roots and spatial relations cannot carry naturally

candidates.concept: specialist astronomical and meteorological classes
candidates.placement: future module vocabulary plus exact scientific source material
candidates.summary: Specialist astronomy can describe comets, asteroids, and galaxies without moving them all into the base path. Atmospheric layers, precipitation classes, and named severe-weather systems can likewise remain exact in their source material.
candidates.revisit_when: an astronomy or meteorology scenario needs the same class repeatedly for explanation rather than only as a named source object

candidates.concept: a single organism or living thing across plants, animals, and fungi
candidates.placement: Ecological or shared base, according to connected use
candidates.summary: `lioru` names life, `lima` states that something is alive, and the familiar living categories retain their own nouns. A generic countable organism term may still help when one participant must range across those categories.
candidates.revisit_when: connected ecological or philosophical writing repeatedly needs a singular participant spanning plants, animals, fungi, and other living categories

candidates.concept: shrub, stem, thorn, sap, and pollen
candidates.summary: These terms recur wherever plants meet hands or bodies: gardens, paths, care, and ecological writing. They separate growth form from supporting axis, then give thorn, sap, and pollen direct names.

candidates.concept: trunk, petal, mushroom, thicket, orchard, and crop
candidates.summary: Each expression leaves the defining plant or fungus relation in view. The six compounds are short in speech, and their parts help a learner recover the meaning.

candidates.concept: specialist plant and fungus structures, exact kinds, and contextual food or cultivation categories
candidates.placement: Ecological, Household, Work, Medical, or exact source material
candidates.summary: This group mixes technical structures and named kinds with categories drawn by cooking, cultivation, or a particular purpose. One decision for all of them would be tidy only on paper.
candidates.revisit_when: connected ecological, garden, food, craft, or care writing repeatedly needs one of these distinctions beyond a transparent description or exact source name

candidates.concept: ordinary animal feet, outer covering, mouth structures, homes, companion relations, young, calls, and groups
candidates.summary: Animal, body part, material quality, or social relation stays audible in each expression. The same small set of words covers familiar feet, homes, groups, and companion relations without a second list of opaque roots.

candidates.concept: specialist animal anatomy, life stages, sex and age classes, breeds, calls, and taxonomic ranks
candidates.placement: Ecological, Medical, a later animal-care vocabulary set, or exact source material
candidates.summary: The group spans body structures, life stages, husbandry classes, and formal taxa, each answering a different practical question. Existing body words cover ordinary description while biological or legal categories keep the records that define them.
candidates.revisit_when: connected ecological, care, husbandry, or literary writing repeatedly needs one of these distinctions and transparent description becomes cumbersome or ambiguous

candidates.concept: plastic, rubber, steel, ceramic, and concrete
candidates.summary: These five broad materials recur in daily life and ecological discussion. Homes, streets, clothing, and repair work all need names for them. Direct nouns keep those sentences short; grade and composition enter only when they matter.

candidates.concept: brick, adhesive, fuel, card or cardboard, and alloy
candidates.summary: Each compound exposes the practical relation behind its category instead of adding five opaque roots. A formulation or grade can join the Phi expression when it matters.

candidates.concept: paint, varnish, mortar, cement, plaster, asphalt, coal, charcoal, and related material products
candidates.placement: Household, Work, Systems, Ecological, or shared base according to connected use
candidates.summary: Paint and coal happen to be materials, but that fact gives them little else in common. Their next connected scenarios can decide which distinctions stay transparent and which deserve direct words.
candidates.revisit_when: a connected household, craft, construction, energy, or ecological scenario repeatedly needs one of these products beyond a transparent material description

candidates.concept: additional ordinary metals, alloys, fibres, and natural or manufactured materials
candidates.placement: base or one or more material-using modules according to connected use
candidates.summary: Aluminium, brass, bronze, cork, and similar names may earn Phi forms through repeated ordinary use. The present batch does not turn a survey of available materials into another list a general speaker must memorise.
candidates.revisit_when: connected use repeatedly needs the same material identity in Phi rather than only in an exact source record

candidates.concept: exact chemical substances, polymer identities, alloy grades, product formulations, and trade names
candidates.placement: exact source material with a Phi account
candidates.summary: A broad Phi noun can still support discussion of a material's handling and effects. It cannot do the technical identifier's job.
candidates.source_rule: The exact identifier stays outside the Phi passage with the notation and version that make it usable. Formulas and grades retain their source forms. So do standards, formulations, and trade identities.

candidates.concept: ceiling, stairs, corridor, gate, fence, and chimney
candidates.summary: Six common building features are short relations among an interior, route, boundary, wall, door, roof, and smoke. The registered compounds fit many building traditions and spare six opaque roots.

candidates.concept: installed cooking, cooling, washing, bathing, and sanitation appliances or fixtures
candidates.placement: Household, Systems, Built Environment, or exact source material according to use
candidates.summary: Broad function can already be described with a device, place, action, temperature, water path, or storage relation. Stove, oven, refrigerator, sink, bath, and similar categories divide differently across technologies and dwellings, so connected use should decide which distinctions deserve Phi forms.
candidates.revisit_when: a household, infrastructure, access, repair, or inhabited-place scenario repeatedly needs one stable appliance or fixture distinction beyond its function and exact source identity

candidates.concept: additional portable, directional, or electrically powered light sources
candidates.placement: base words and descriptions with exact equipment records where needed
candidates.summary: `luma` names the general made light source, `lumoi` a portable protected lamp, and `philu` a wick-fed candle. Shape, beam, energy source, and controls can distinguish a torch, flashlight, or electric fitting without another general light noun.

candidates.concept: additional utensils, vessels, furnishings, and functional garment types
candidates.placement: Household, base, or another practical module according to connected use
candidates.summary: The base layer and Household module already cover ordinary containers, seating, bedding, storage, cleaning, clothing, and tableware. Forks, specialised pans, cupboards, and narrower footwear can be described by function until one distinction repeatedly earns a direct word.
candidates.revisit_when: connected daily-life, care, craft, or literary use repeatedly needs one practical object or garment type and the transparent description becomes cumbersome or ambiguous

candidates.concept: exact food, dish, garment, household-object, and product identities
candidates.placement: exact source identity beside a Phi account
candidates.summary: Phi can describe how an item is made and used, what it is made from, and how it relates to the scene. A familiar category may still receive a Phi root later; exact identity remains with the source that establishes it.
candidates.source_rule: An original name, cultural designation, or model stays in its source form when identity depends on it. Recipes, labels, and safety instructions remain with that source while Phi describes the item's ordinary relations and effects.

candidates.concept: additional hand tools, fasteners, powered tools, protective equipment, and calibration equipment
candidates.placement: Household, Work, Systems, Accessibility, base, or exact source material according to connected use
candidates.summary: The base inventory supplies a general tool and device beside several familiar hand implements. This deferred field includes further cutting and digging tools, fasteners, powered equipment, and protective or calibration gear. Those groups answer different practical questions, so one undifferentiated expansion would be larger than it is useful.
candidates.revisit_when: connected household, craft, access, repair, or technical work repeatedly needs one stable implement or equipment class beyond its function and exact source identity

candidates.concept: ordinary tool and device parts such as handle, blade, head, grip, and fastener
candidates.placement: base descriptions and Work or Systems compounds
candidates.summary: A part can be named by the contact or material change it performs, while its shape, material, and place in the larger object remain open. Exact replacement parts and standardized fasteners retain the identity supplied by their technical source.

candidates.concept: screens, filters, grids, and exact mesh classes beyond an ordinary net
candidates.placement: Household, Ecological, Systems, Work, or exact source material according to function
candidates.summary: Base `mera` covers flexible open mesh and its ordinary physical uses. A rigid screen, fluid filter, computational grid, and measured mesh grade have little in common beyond an English family resemblance.
candidates.revisit_when: connected household, ecological, craft, or technical use repeatedly needs one mesh-based object apart from its material, openings, function, and source specification

candidates.concept: chimes, alarms, and other audible alert devices
candidates.placement: base words with Systems vocabulary or exact source records where needed
candidates.summary: `teli` names the resonant bell or chime, while `kirowi` names a detected signal and `pukea` can state danger. A technical alarm account can add the source and sound, then say who should notice it and how they interpret it.

candidates.concept: road, trail, walkway, and other traversable ground
candidates.summary: Base `ruela` now states its ordinary range plainly: worn footpath, trail, made walkway, and prepared road are all traversable lines. Surface, construction, condition, permitted users, and access can be described without a separate road root.

candidates.concept: a general vehicle or transport device
candidates.summary: `phaero keli` identifies an object by its transport function without claiming a particular power source, wheel arrangement, ownership relation, or service. Direct boat and wagon nouns remain available when their form is already known.

candidates.concept: cycles, powered road vehicles, public transport vehicles, aircraft, and other transport classes
candidates.placement: Systems, Accessibility, Built Environment, Ecological, base, or exact source material according to connected use
candidates.summary: A bicycle and bus do not ask the same thing of a route, body, energy source, or shared service, and an aircraft differs again. Existing vocabulary can describe their movement, construction, carrying role, and source of energy until one class repeatedly needs a direct Phi word.
candidates.revisit_when: a connected mobility, access, infrastructure, ecological, repair, or literary scenario repeatedly loses one transport distinction in transparent description

candidates.concept: exact vehicle models, routes, services, navigation identifiers, and equipment specifications
candidates.placement: exact source identity beside a Phi account
candidates.summary: Phi can describe the vehicle, route, present condition, access relation, and travel event around an exact identifier. The identifier itself remains in the map, timetable, ticket, or technical record that makes it usable.
candidates.source_rule: Preserve model and service names, route numbers, and navigation codes in their original source form. Schedules, dimensions, ratings, and safety specifications keep their source notation.

candidates.concept: east and west within the cardinal direction system
candidates.summary: Base `nitho` and `ronua` name north and south. East and west stay tied to the daily solar course: the sun's beginning names east, and its end names west. The four directions remain ordinary, value-neutral nouns.

candidates.concept: corners, bends, and intersections
candidates.summary: English corner covers several shapes. Two joined edges or walls make one kind, a path's turn makes another, and connected paths make a third. Keeping those structures visible is clearer than asking one root to hide all three.

candidates.concept: geometric points, lines, angles, axes, polygons, and exact relations beyond ordinary shape
candidates.placement: base, Work, Systems, a future mathematics profile, or exact source material according to connected use
candidates.summary: Circle, straightness, edges, surfaces, dimensions, position, and spatial relators cover ordinary physical description. A technical geometry inventory would introduce defined objects and relations whose exact meanings should be chosen together rather than borrowed one English headword at a time.
candidates.revisit_when: connected mathematical, design, craft, engineering, or teaching work repeatedly needs stable geometric objects and relations inside Phi rather than in an accompanying diagram or source definition

candidates.concept: exact coordinates, addresses, map references, parcel identifiers, and survey records
candidates.placement: exact source identity beside a Phi account
candidates.summary: Phi can discuss an exact spatial record through its ordinary spatial vocabulary. The record keeps the notation and reference system that lets another person locate or verify it.
candidates.source_rule: Coordinates, addresses, grid references, parcel and site identifiers, survey marks, datum names, scales, and exact dimensions retain their original source form. Phi may describe their place, use, source, and uncertainty without recoding them.

candidates.concept: language varieties, dialects, registers, accents, grammar, scripts, and bounded sentences
candidates.summary: A language form, style, or voice colour says which kind of variation matters. Language pattern supplies grammar, writing pattern supplies script, and the event noun of `shemui` supplies a bounded utterance or sentence.

candidates.concept: exact named languages, scripts, orthographies, linguistic classifications, and source wording
candidates.placement: source identity beside a Phi account or an accepted Phi-form onym
candidates.summary: Phi can discuss how people use and transmit a language, including interpretation across languages or modes, without recoding the identity that another community supplies. An accepted Phi-form onym may name a language or script, but it does not replace the source name.
candidates.source_rule: Preserve exact language and script names, orthographies, linguistic classifications, and quoted source wording in their original source form. A bearer or relevant naming community may accept a Phi-form onym under the ordinary name charter.

candidates.concept: poems, paintings, drawings, sculptures, novels, genres, and performances
candidates.summary: Phi names a work through the act or form that made it. `melira` covers poem and song, `kire` supplies drawing or shaping as an event noun, and `nophi shelu` identifies a story book. Singing, dancing, and playing retain the specific performance instead of entering one general genre hierarchy.

candidates.concept: artist, writer, reader, singer, dancer, composer, and performer roles
candidates.placement: base relative clauses beside existing role nouns
candidates.summary: Direct nouns already name musician, poet, and storyteller. Other roles use a relative clause whose verb states the person's actual work. This also lets a role belong to one occasion without turning it into a permanent class.

candidates.concept: a general musical instrument beyond drum, flute, bell, and instrument string
candidates.summary: `meliphe tenoa` identifies an object by its musical work. Base nouns still name the drum, flute, bell, and tensioned string when those familiar forms matter.

candidates.concept: additional instrument families, musical notation, melody, harmony theory, metre, tuning, and technical performance vocabulary
candidates.placement: base, a future music vocabulary set, or exact source material according to connected use
candidates.summary: The current lexicon distinguishes sound from music, a song from singing, and rhythm from harmony. It also has a general play verb, direct nouns for drum and flute, and words for a bell and a tensioned string when those forms matter. Music teaching or sustained practice may still need narrower relations chosen together rather than a shelf of borrowed labels.
candidates.revisit_when: connected musical composition, teaching, instrument care, or performance repeatedly needs stable distinctions that the present sound, timing, relation, and source vocabulary cannot carry naturally

candidates.concept: exact work titles, quotations, lyrics, scores, named styles, genre labels, and source identifiers
candidates.placement: source material beside a Phi account or an accepted Phi-form onym
candidates.summary: Phi can name the kind of work, its maker, medium, form, and reception. Exact identity stays with the title, wording, notation, or catalogue record that establishes which work is meant.
candidates.source_rule: Preserve exact titles, quotations, lyrics, scores, named styles, genre labels, catalogue identifiers, and edition details in their source form. A work may also bear an accepted Phi-form onym under `ne`, without changing the preserved source material.

prompt_sources.title: Concepticon: Dunn 2012 207-item list
prompt_sources.note: Used as a cross-linguistic prompt for common concepts, not as a list Phi must lexicalise.

prompt_sources.title: SIL Comparative Lists of Semantic Domains, version 4
prompt_sources.note: Used to widen the audit beyond the headings that the earlier migration happened to choose.
